context: Tests whether the agent identifies the full set of anti-patterns present in a flawed architecture proposal: unjustified technology, copy-paste architecture from large tech companies, over-engineering relative to team size, missing NFRs, and vague deferrals — without simply approving the proposal.

checklist.name: Flags unjustified technology
checklist.description: architecture-review.md flags Kafka (or the event streaming choice) as mentioned without justification beyond citing Netflix/Uber

checklist.name: Asks for alternatives
checklist.description: architecture-review.md asks what alternatives to the chosen technology were considered

checklist.name: Flags over-engineering
checklist.description: architecture-review.md explicitly flags the mismatch between 14 services and a team of 3, questioning whether all components are necessary

checklist.name: Flags missing availability NFR
checklist.description: architecture-review.md flags that availability or uptime requirements are absent or marked TBD

checklist.name: Flags missing security NFR
checklist.description: architecture-review.md flags that security or compliance requirements (e.g. HIPAA) are deferred without a plan

checklist.name: Flags missing monitoring NFR
checklist.description: architecture-review.md flags that monitoring or alerting requirements are absent or vague

checklist.name: Challenges deferral
checklist.description: architecture-review.md challenges the security/HIPAA deferral with a specific question about the worst-case impact or quantifies the risk of deferring

checklist.name: Challenges copy-paste
checklist.description: architecture-review.md questions whether the architecture was chosen because it resembles large-scale companies (Netflix, Uber, Airbnb) rather than because it fits the team's actual requirements

checklist.name: Does not approve
checklist.description: architecture-review.md does NOT validate or endorse the proposal without raising substantive concerns
